claims.claim: At equivalence ratio φ = 0.5, N2 concentration needs to exceed 50% to completely inhibit BVG explosion, while CO2 only needs to exceed 30%.
claims.quote: When φ = 0.5, the concentration of N2 needs to be more than 50% to completely inhibit the explosion of the BVG reaction, while CO2 only needs to be more than 30%.

claims.claim: At equivalence ratio φ = 0.8, increasing CO2 concentration from 5% to 25% reduced Pmax from 0.53 MPa to 0.28 MPa (a 47.17% reduction), reduced (dp/dt)max from 21 MPa/s to 1.8 MPa/s (a 91.43% reduction), and increased tex from 83 ms to 570 ms (a 586.75% increase).
claims.quote: The concentration of CO2 increased from 5% to 25%, at which time the Pmax was reduced from 0.53 MPa to 0.28 MPa, a reduction of 47.17%; (dp/dt)max was reduced from 21 MPa/s to 1.8 MPa/s, a reduction of 91.43%; tex increased from 83 ms to 570 ms, with an increase of 586.75%.

claims.claim: Under jet conditions at φ = 0.5, the presence of an air jet increased Pex from 0.1 MPa to 0.37 MPa, increased dp/dt from 0.35 MPa/s to 8.2 MPa/s, and decreased tex from 746 ms to 135 ms.
claims.quote: When φ = 0.5, the jet condition has a greater enhancement of the explosion hazard of BVG. Pex increased from 0.1 MPa to 0.37 MPa, dp/dt increased from 0.35 MPa/s to 8.2 MPa/s, and tex decreased from 746 ms to 135 ms in the presence of air jet.

claims.claim: Under premixed conditions at φ = 0.5, a CO2 volume concentration exceeding 30% completely inhibits BVG explosion, but under jet conditions the CO2 concentration must exceed 35% to achieve complete inhibition.
claims.quote: Under premixed conditions, a volume concentration of CO2 of more than 30% could completely inhibit the explosion of BVG. However, under the jet effect, the concentration of CO2 needs to exceed 35% to completely inhibit the explosion of BVG.

claims.claim: At φ = 0.6 with 100 g/m3 graphite dust, the inhibition effect of CO2 on hybrid explosion (maximum reduced Pex of 0.28 MPa, ratio 46.7%) is better than the inhibition effect of CO2 jet on BVG alone (maximum reduced Pex of 0.12 MPa, ratio 27.3%), because unburned graphite dust absorbs heat and CO2 reduces oxygen availability for graphite combustion.
claims.quote: the inhibition effect of CO2 on the explosion of hybrids is better than the inhibition effect of CO2 jet on the explosion of BVG. This is because in addition to the inert mechanism of CO2 on BVG, a large amount of unburned graphite is produced. These unburned graphite dust also absorb the heat of the BVG explosion.
